   <item>
      <title>Bucks To Hire Taylor Jenkins As Head Coach</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285261/Bucks-To-Hire-Taylor-Jenkins-As-Head-Coach</link>
      <description><p>Taylor Jenkins is nearing agreement to become the next head coach of the Milwaukee Bucks. Jenkins was an assistant coach with the Bucks during the 2018-19 season under Mike Budenholzer before becoming head coach of the Memphis Grizzlies.</p>
<p>Jenkins, who will replace Doc Rivers, spent the 2025-26 season outside of basketball following his dismissal as head coach of the Grizzlies at the end of the 2024-25 season. The Bucks met with Jenkins in Memphis last week and appeared to be their top target.</p>
<p>The Bucks are navigating the future of Giannis Antetokounmpo as they evaluate whether they will continue to try to build around him or trade him and reset their roster.</p>
<p>Jenkins was considered the top available coach during this hiring cycle.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 15:44:50 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285261/Bucks-To-Hire-Taylor-Jenkins-As-Head-Coach</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jenkins_Taylor_mem_220429.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Williams Ruled Out Indefinitely With Grade 1 Hamstring Strain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285262/Jalen-Williams-Ruled-Out-Indefinitely-With-Grade-1-Hamstring-Strain</link>
      <description><p>Oklahoma City Thunder forward Jalen Williams will miss at least the next three games of the team's first-round series against the Phoenix Suns after sustaining a Grade 1 left hamstring strain, the team announced Thursday. Williams will be evaluated on a weekly basis.</p>
<p>The injury occurred with 6:26 remaining in the third quarter of Wednesday's 120-107 Game 2 victory. Williams grabbed his left leg after missing a fast-break layup attempt, subsequently took a deliberate foul to exit the game and went directly to the locker room. He did not address reporters before departing Paycom Center.</p>
<p>Williams is ruled out for Saturday's Game 3, Monday's Game 4 and a potential Wednesday Game 5.</p>
<p>The timing is a significant blow despite Oklahoma City's commanding series position. Williams had been outstanding to open the postseason after a regular season limited to 33 games due to injury. He posted 22 points, seven rebounds and six assists in Game 1 and followed with 19 points on 7-of-11 shooting in 23 minutes in Game 2.</p>
<p>"He was playing an outstanding game," coach Mark Daigneault said. "He was on the gas. He had great force. He was hitting shots."</p>
<p>Williams, a third-team All-NBA selection last season, played through a torn scapholunate ligament in his right wrist throughout last year's championship run before undergoing surgery. He also missed extended stretches this season with a right hamstring strain.</p>
<p>Oklahoma City went 39-10 without Williams in the lineup this regular season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 16:08:46 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285262/Jalen-Williams-Ruled-Out-Indefinitely-With-Grade-1-Hamstring-Strain</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Williams_Jalen_okc_250531.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Giannis Antetokounmpo Hasn't Communicated With Bucks' Upper Brass Since Before Deadline</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285263/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Hasnt-Communicated-With-Bucks-Upper-Brass-Since-Before-Deadline</link>
      <description><p>Giannis Antetokounmpo has not had any substantive dialogue with the Milwaukee Bucks' upper brass since the trade deadline, sources tell ESPN. Antetokounmpo was also not believed to have any involvement in <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285261/Bucks-To-Hire-Taylor-Jenkins-As-Head-Coach">the Bucks' decision to hire Taylor Jenkins as head coach</a>. Jenkins was an assistant coach under Mike Budenholzer earlier in Antetokounmpo's career.</p>
<p>The future of Antetokounmpo is one of the NBA's most pressing questions this offseason as he approaches an inflection point with the franchise. Antetokounmpo becomes eligible to sign up to a four-year, $275 million extension with the Bucks in October.</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo did not close the door to the possibility of remaining with the Bucks when asked whether he's played his final game with the franchise.</p>
<p>"That's a very good question," Antetokounmpo said. "I don't know. It's not up to me. We'll see."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 18:15:32 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285263/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Hasnt-Communicated-With-Bucks-Upper-Brass-Since-Before-Deadline</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250502.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>CJ McCollum's Late Fadeaway Lifts Hawks Over Knicks 109-108 To Take Game 3</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285268/CJ-McCollums-Late-Fadeaway-Lifts-Hawks-Over-Knicks-109-108-To-Take-Game-3</link>
      <description><p>CJ McCollum converted a 15-foot fadeaway jumper with 12.5 seconds remaining Thursday night, giving the Atlanta Hawks a 109-108 victory over the New York Knicks in Game 3. Atlanta leads the first-round series 2-1 heading into Saturday's Game 4.</p>
<p>McCollum, who tormented New York in Game 2 at Madison Square Garden, finished with 23 points. Jalen Johnson paced the Hawks with 24 points while Jonathan Kuminga contributed 21 off the bench.</p>
<p>"They work, they share," said Hawks coach Quin Snyder. "That's the thing about this group that I've enjoyed so much."</p>
<p>Atlanta led by as many as 18 points in the first half before New York mounted a late charge. Jalen Brunson's 3-point play gave the Knicks a 108-105 edge with 1:03 remaining. Jalen Johnson answered with a basket, cutting the deficit to one and setting up McCollum's decisive shot.</p>
<p>New York had two opportunities to win at the end but came up empty. Josh Hart missed a 3-pointer, the Knicks failed to beat the shot clock on the offensive rebound, and Brunson lost the ball to Kuminga as time expired.</p>
<p>OG Anunoby led New York with 29 points. Brunson added 26 and Karl-Anthony Towns contributed 21.</p>
<p>"You couldn't ask for anything better than that," Knicks coach Mike Brown said of the late-game situation. "Less than a minute to go and a chance to go up by three."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Knicks are -2.5 point favorites in Game 4 at Atlanta on Saturday</a>. The Hawks have jumped to the favorites to win the series with implied odds of 57 percent.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 23:54:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285268/CJ-McCollums-Late-Fadeaway-Lifts-Hawks-Over-Knicks-109-108-To-Take-Game-3</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/McCollum_CJ_atl_260116.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Victor Wembanyama Traveling With Spurs To Portland, Status For Game 3 Uncertain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285259/Victor-Wembanyama-Traveling-With-Spurs-To-Portland-Status-For-Game-3-Uncertain</link>
      <description><p>Victor Wembanyama will accompany the San Antonio Spurs to Portland this weekend while continuing to work through the NBA's concussion protocol. Coach Mitch Johnson described the center as "progressing" but stopped short of clearing him for Game 3 Friday against the Portland Trail Blazers.</p>
<p>Wembanyama sustained the concussion during Tuesday's Game 2 loss to Portland, exiting in the second quarter. The series is tied 1-1, with Game 3 on Friday and Game 4 on Sunday, both in Portland. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Spurs are -3.5 point favorites for Game 3</a>.</p>
<p>The NBA's return-to-play protocol requires players to complete sequential steps, beginning with light exertion such as stationary cycling, jogging, agility work and non-contact drills, each followed by a neurological examination. Results are measured against a pre-season baseline evaluation before clearance is granted.</p>
<p>The 7-foot-4 French center, who became <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285201/Victor-Wembanyama-Unanimously-Named-NBA-Defensive-Player-Of-The-Year">the league's first unanimous Defensive Player of the Year</a> and is among three MVP finalists, anchors everything the Spurs do. San Antonio finished with the NBA's second-best regular season record and went 12-6 in games without him.</p>
<p>Wembanyama averaged 25 points, 11.5 rebounds, 3.1 assists and a league-leading 3.1 blocks per game this season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 12:51:44 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285259/Victor-Wembanyama-Traveling-With-Spurs-To-Portland-Status-For-Game-3-Uncertain</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_260226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Karl-Anthony Towns Wants To Be 'A Knick For Life'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285250/Karl-Anthony-Towns-Wants-To-Be-A-Knick-For-Life</link>
      <description><p>Karl-Anthony Towns has expressed a strong desire to remain with the New York Knicks long-term, according to a source close to the big man. However, his postseason performance and fit within coach Mike Brown's system are drawing sharp scrutiny around the league.</p>
<p>"He so badly wants things to work here," a source close to Towns told ESPN. "He wants to be a Knick for life."</p>
<p>Despite that desire, rival executives warn Towns faces significant pressure. The Knicks, under a direct mandate from owner James Dolan, are expected to reach <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/New-York-Knicks/20/playoff-history">the franchise's first NBA Finals since 1999</a>.</p>
<p>Towns carries two years remaining on his contract, worth $57.7 million in 2026-27 with a $61 million player option for 2027-28. That salary makes him New York's largest potential trade asset should the organization pursue Milwaukee Bucks forward Giannis Antetokounmpo.</p>
<p>Towns had interest in extending before the start of the current season, but didn't expect the Knicks to offer one.</p>
<p>League sources indicate the Bucks and Knicks held exclusive negotiations centered on Antetokounmpo last summer. With Milwaukee's turbulent season, further exploration of the two-time MVP's future is widely anticipated.</p>
<p>The way Towns is utilized remains a persistent debate. His 13.8 shot attempts this regular season marked a career low, while scoring dropped to 20.1 points per game, his lowest since his rookie year. Several Eastern Conference assistants have questioned how Brown's staff has deployed him.</p>
<p>The stakes for Towns personally are considerable.</p>
<p>"If they come up short, if they don't make the Finals -- aside from Bridges, because he was traded for all the draft picks -- Towns will take the most heat because of his resume," a high-ranking rival team executive said. "Because Brunson won't take the heat and they'll say Towns didn't deliver. Especially with Giannis sitting out there."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 10:05:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285250/Karl-Anthony-Towns-Wants-To-Be-A-Knick-For-Life</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Towns_Karlanthony_nyk_250429.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>AJ Dybantsa Declares For 2026 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285253/AJ-Dybantsa-Declares-For-2026-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>AJ Dybantsa has declared for the 2026 NBA Draft, leaving BYU after his freshman season.</p>
<p>Dybantsa is one of the leading candidates to be selected with the No. 1 overall pick. Dybantsa is believed to have surpassed Darryn Peterson atop the big boards of many NBA teams due to his higher floor.</p>
<p>Dybantsa averaged 25.5 points, 6.8 rebounds and 3.7 assists in 34.8 minutes across 35 games for BYU.</p>
<p>Dybantsa was a Consensus All-American First Team selection and the Big 12 Freshman of the Year.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 11:52:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285253/AJ-Dybantsa-Declares-For-2026-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dybantsa_AJ_byu_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Steve Kerr Decision Hasn't Been Made As Internal Optimism Wanes</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285254/Steve-Kerr-Decision-Hasnt-Been-Made-As-Internal-Optimism-Wanes</link>
      <description><p>Optimism within the Golden State Warriors organization about Steve Kerr's return has dimmed in recent weeks, multiple team sources told The Athletic. Staffers have begun preparing for a potential coaching change, though sources stressed no decision is final until Kerr meets with owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy.</p>
<p>ESPN reported Monday that the Warriors would require Kerr to sign a multi-year extension if he returns. One team source neither confirmed nor denied that detail. Speculation around the league regarding possible successors has already surfaced.</p>
<p>League and team sources said Kerr's desire to speak openly on social and political matters caused internal friction at points throughout the season. Veteran journalist Marc Spears said this week that Kerr has felt "stifled" at times, though whether that factor proves decisive remains uncertain.</p>
<p>Stephen Curry, who will earn nearly $63 million next season and indicated openness to a contract extension, declined to publicly urge Kerr to stay.</p>
<p>"I want Coach to be happy," Curry said. "I want him to be excited about the job. Whatever that means for him, everybody's plan is their own."</p>
<p>Kerr has been transparent that he has a strong desire to continue coaching Curry.</p>
<p>"I don't want to walk away from Steph," Kerr said following Friday's season-ending loss to the Phoenix Suns. "I'm definitely not going and coaching somewhere else next year in the NBA. I would never walk away from Steph, but all the stuff has to be aligned and right."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 12:07:04 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285254/Steve-Kerr-Decision-Hasnt-Been-Made-As-Internal-Optimism-Wanes</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_211228.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joel Embiid Upgraded To Doubtful For Game 3</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285264/Joel-Embiid-Upgraded-To-Doubtful-For-Game-3</link>
      <description><p>Joel Embiid has been upgraded to doubtful by the Philadelphia 76ers ahead of Game 3 on Friday against the Boston Celtics. Embiid underwent emergency appendectomy two weeks ago.</p>
<p>Embiid began a strength and conditioning program Monday and then participated in parts of practice Thursday.</p>
<p>"We're, obviously, really hopeful, and I think he's really hopeful," 76ers coach Nick Nurse told reporters after Thursday's practice. "He wants to play, and we want him to play.</p>
<p>"I don't know how close we are to that at this point, but I think there's some progress being made."</p>
<p>The Sixers evened the series 1-1 with a Game 2 win at the Celtics led by impressive performances by VJ Edgecombe and Tyrese Maxey.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 18:19:23 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285264/Joel-Embiid-Upgraded-To-Doubtful-For-Game-3</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Embiid_Joel_phi_260406.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Austin Reaves Upgraded To Questionable For Game 3</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285266/Austin-Reaves-Upgraded-To-Questionable-For-Game-3</link>
      <description><p>The Los Angeles Lakers have upgraded Austin Reaves to questionable for Game 3 of their first round series against the Houston Rockets as he works his way back from a Grade 2 left oblique strain.</p>
<p>Reaves suffered the injury on April 2nd during a blowout loss at the Oklahoma City Thunder. Reaves began his return-to-play program this week.</p>
<p>Reaves averaged a career-best 23.3 points during the regular season.</p>
<p>The Lakers have a 2-0 series lead over the Rockets as the series shifts to Houston.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 21:09:22 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285266/Austin-Reaves-Upgraded-To-Questionable-For-Game-3</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Reaves_Austin_lal_251116.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Devin Booker Fined $35,000 For Criticism Of Refs, NBA Rescinds Technical</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285267/Devin-Booker-Fined-$35000-For-Criticism-Of-Refs-NBA-Rescinds-Technical</link>
      <description><p>Phoenix Suns guard Devin Booker has been fined $35,000 for public criticism of the officiating. Following an investigation including multiple interviews and video review, the league found no basis to any claim of bias or misconduct by game officials.</p>
<p>Booker made his comments to the press following the Suns' 120-107 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 2 of their first-round playoff series on April 22 at Paycom Center.</p>
<p>"In my 11 years, I haven't called a ref out by name, but James [Williams] was terrible tonight through and through," <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285249/Devin-Booker-Dillon-Brooks-Blast-Officiating-After-Suns-Game-2-Loss-To-Thunder">Booker said on Wednesday</a>. "It's bad for the sport, bad for the integrity of the sport. People are going to start viewing this as a WWE if they're not held responsible."</p>
<p>The NBA also determined that Booker's technical foul at 2:05 in the third quarter was improperly assessed and it has therefore been rescinded.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 21:55:59 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285267/Devin-Booker-Fined-$35000-For-Criticism-Of-Refs-NBA-Rescinds-Technical</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Booker_Devin_phx_241227.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Aaron Gordon Misses Game 3 With Calf Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285265/Aaron-Gordon-Misses-Game-3-With-Calf-Injury</link>
      <description><p>Aaron Gordon was held out by the Denver Nuggets for Game 3 on Thursday with calf tightness. Gordon was listed as probable initially on the injury report, but was downgraded to questionable on Thursday morning and then officially ruled out two hours before the start of the game.</p>
<p>Gordon's calf issue that flared up in Game 2 didn't improve in time for Thursday.</p>
<p>The Nuggets were without Gordon for 46 games this season due to repetitive hamstring issues. Denver is also playing their first round series against the Minnesota Timberwolves due to a hamstring strain.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 20:34:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285265/Aaron-Gordon-Misses-Game-3-With-Calf-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Gordon_Aaron_den_230109.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Derrick White Wins 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285255/Derrick-White-Wins-2025-26-NBA-Sportsmanship-Award</link>
      <description><p>Boston Celtics guard Derrick White has been selected as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/NBA-Sportsmanship-Award/36">the 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award recipient</a>. White claims the Joe Dumars Trophy for the first time in his nine-year career.</p>
<p>The honor marks consecutive seasons in which a Celtic has earned the award as Jrue Holiday received the trophy last season. While Holiday is a two-time winner, Mike Conley has won the award four times.</p>
<p>Current NBA players cast votes to determine the winner from six divisional finalists. A panel of league executives selected those finalists from 30 team nominees, one per club.</p>
<p>The Joe Dumars Trophy has been presented annually since the 1995-96 season. It honors the player who best exemplifies sportsmanship on the court and is named for Naismith Basketball Hall of Famer Joe Dumars, who won the inaugural award and spent his entire 14-year career with the Detroit Pistons.</p>
<p>White brings a decorated resume to the recognition. A two-time NBA All-Defensive Team selection, he won an NBA championship with Boston in 2023-24 and captured a gold medal with the USA Men's National Team at the 2024 Paris Olympics. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 12:09:59 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285255/Derrick-White-Wins-2025-26-NBA-Sportsmanship-Award</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/White_Derrick_bos_250421.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Devin Booker, Dillon Brooks Blast Officiating After Suns' Game 2 Loss To Thunder</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285249/Devin-Booker-Dillon-Brooks-Blast-Officiating-After-Suns-Game-2-Loss-To-Thunder</link>
      <description><p>Devin Booker publicly criticized a referee by name following Phoenix's 120-107 Game 2 defeat to Oklahoma City on Wednesday. The loss gives the defending champion Thunder a commanding 2-0 first-round series lead.</p>
<p>"In my 11 years, I haven't called a ref out by name, but James [Williams] was terrible tonight through and through," Booker said. "It's bad for the sport, bad for the integrity of the sport. People are going to start viewing this as a WWE if they're not held responsible."</p>
<p>Booker took particular issue with a technical foul assessed against him at 2:05 of the third quarter, which he said came without explanation.</p>
<p>"This is my first time in 11 years, but it's needed," Booker said. "Whatever I get fined for it, everybody can pull the clips and see where the frustration is from."</p>
<p>Forward Dillon Brooks echoed the complaints, directing his criticism at foul calls benefiting reigning MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander. Brooks scored 30 points before fouling out late. Gilgeous-Alexander led all scorers with 37 points, connecting on all nine free throw attempts.</p>
<p>"[Gilgeous-Alexander is] a little frail, and that's what the refs are going to call," Brooks said. "This is the playoffs. It's a man's game."</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander deflected the criticism gracefully while praising Brooks as a competitor.</p>
<p>"I can't control what Dillon or anybody else on the other side is going to complain about," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "All I can do is try to go out there and win basketball games for my team."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/">Game 3 shifts to Phoenix with the Suns 9.5 point underdogs against the defending champion Thunder</a>. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 07:27:59 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285249/Devin-Booker-Dillon-Brooks-Blast-Officiating-After-Suns-Game-2-Loss-To-Thunder</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Booker_Devin_phx_231028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Pistons End 11-Game Home Playoff Losing Streak With Dominant Game 2 Win Over Magic</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285248/Pistons-End-11-Game-Home-Playoff-Losing-Streak-With-Dominant-Game-2-Win-Over-Magic</link>
      <description><p>A fierce halftime speech from J.B. Bickerstaff sparked the Detroit Pistons as they had a 30-3 third-quarter run to defeat the Orlando Magic 98-83, leveling the first-round series at 1-1 on Wednesday night.</p>
<p>The victory ended Detroit's NBA-record 11-game home playoff losing streak. The Pistons' last home postseason win had come on May 26, 2008, against Boston in the Eastern Conference finals.</p>
<p>"He really got on us in the locker room," said Tobias Harris. "[His message was] there is no more of 'my bads.' It's like they're out there hustling, getting offensive boards on us. And there's too many of them for us [to allow] as a group. We know that's not our standard. So he was on us. We were able to find that little spark."</p>
<p>Detroit's defense proved decisive. The Pistons recorded 11 total blocks and held Orlando to just 18% shooting on contested attempts, the lowest such figure in postseason play since ESPN began tracking the stat in 2013-14.</p>
<p>Cade Cunningham led Detroit, finishing with 27 points, 11 assists and six rebounds after a 39-point showing in Game 1.</p>
<p>"It's Piston's basketball, and that's what it looks like," Bickerstaff said. "We have one off night [in Game 1 that happened] to come at a bad time. But I know what our guys are, and we trusted that they were going to come back tonight, be the best version of themselves at some point."</p>
<p>Orlando's Paolo Banchero tallied 18 points, eight assists and six rebounds despite the loss.</p>
<p>"We're not backing down," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said. "When we go to Orlando, we got to just make sure we exceed it."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/">Game 3 tips off Saturday in Orlando with the Pistons 3.5 point favorites over the Magic</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 07:24:22 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285248/Pistons-End-11-Game-Home-Playoff-Losing-Streak-With-Dominant-Game-2-Win-Over-Magic</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cunningham_Cade_det_250423.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Williams Exits Game 2 Win Over Suns With Left Hamstring Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285247/Jalen-Williams-Exits-Game-2-Win-Over-Suns-With-Left-Hamstring-Injury</link>
      <description><p>Oklahoma City Thunder wing Jalen Williams left Wednesday night's 120-107 Game 2 victory over the Phoenix Suns with an apparent left hamstring injury in the third quarter and did not return, casting uncertainty over the remainder of the first-round series.</p>
<p>"We think he aggravated his left hamstring," Mark Daigneault said. "We'll take a look at it in the next couple of days and update you guys appropriately."</p>
<p>Williams grabbed his left leg after missing a contested layup on a fast break with 6:26 remaining in the third quarter, appeared to mouth the words "Left hammy," and intentionally drew a foul shortly after to exit the game. He went directly to the locker room and did not speak with media before leaving Paycom Center.</p>
<p>Williams had been exceptional through two postseason games after injuries restricted him to just 33 regular-season appearances. He posted 22 points, seven rebounds, and six assists in Game 1, then added 19 points on 7-of-11 shooting and four assists in 23 minutes before exiting Wednesday.</p>
<p>"Any assumption about time missed is just hypothetical at this point, so I'm not going to comment on that," Daigneault said. "He was playing an outstanding game."</p>
<p>Williams, a third-team All-NBA selection last season, played through a torn scapholunate ligament in his right wrist during Oklahoma City's championship run before undergoing surgery. He missed the first 19 regular-season games recovering from that procedure and twice sat out extended stretches this season with a right hamstring strain.</p>
<p>Oklahoma City leads the series 2-0. Game 3 is Saturday in Phoenix with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball/">the Thunder favored by 9.5 points over the Suns</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 06:54:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285247/Jalen-Williams-Exits-Game-2-Win-Over-Suns-With-Left-Hamstring-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Williams_Jalen_okc_250531.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Execs Believe Caleb Wilson Could Be Second Pick In Upcoming Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285246/Execs-Believe-Caleb-Wilson-Could-Be-Second-Pick-In-Upcoming-Draft</link>
      <description><p>Executives around the league believe that North Carolina's Caleb Wilson could be the second pick in the draft, according to Kevin O'Connor of Yahoo! Sports.</p>
<p>Wilson is viewed as having a similar upside to Kansas' Darryn Peterson but without the downside concerns of Peterson's availability issues.</p>
<p>It was reported earlier in April that <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284877/Caleb-Wilson-Could-Vault-Into-Top-Three-Despite-Injury-Plagued-Freshman-Season">Wilson could enter the top three</a>, ahead of Cameron Boozer.</p>
<p>Wilson averaged 19.8 points and 9.4 rebounds for UNC last season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 02:33:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285246/Execs-Believe-Caleb-Wilson-Could-Be-Second-Pick-In-Upcoming-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Caleb_unc_251223.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Victor Wembanyama Participates In Cardio Work, Game 3 Status Remains In Jeopardy Following Concussion</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285245/Victor-Wembanyama-Participates-In-Cardio-Work-Game-3-Status-Remains-In-Jeopardy-Following-Concussion</link>
      <description><p>ESPN's Shams Charania reported that San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama was able to participate in cardio work on Wednesday as he recovers from a <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285229/Victor-Wembanyama-Suffers-Concussion-After-Hitting-Floor-In-Game-2-Loss">concussion suffered in Game 2</a>, though his status for Friday's Game 3 against the Portland Trail Blazers remains in jeopardy.</p>
<p>"Victor Wembanyama was able to participate in cardio work ... his symptoms didn't worsen, he wasn't feeling worse than he did when he suffered the concussion. As of right now, my understanding is he is hopeful that he'll be able to travel with the team to Portland on Thursday," said Charania.</p>
<p>Charania added that there is no guarantee that Wembanyama will be cleared for Friday's game as he still has to check boxes from both the Spurs and the league surrounding his return. </p>
<p>The league's policy states that Wembanyama will be able to return to basketball activities after 48 hours following a diagnosis.</p>
<p>"What we know about the Spurs is that they're always going to error on the side of caution ... the Spurs are taking this day by day. We'll know more on Thursday about whether he'll be able to play on Friday," added Charania.</p>
<p>The series is tied at one heading into Friday's game in Portland. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 02:17:56 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285245/Victor-Wembanyama-Participates-In-Cardio-Work-Game-3-Status-Remains-In-Jeopardy-Following-Concussion</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_260226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>WNBA To Broadcast Record 216 Games Nationally, Including All 44 Fever Games</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285244/WNBA-To-Broadcast-Record-216-Games-Nationally-Including-All-44-Fever-Games</link>
      <description><p>The WNBA will broadcast a record 216 games and tentpole events for the upcoming season, the league announced on Wednesday.</p>
<p>All 44 Indiana Fever games will also be broadcasted nationally, with the Wings in second place with 36 national games.</p>
<p>The Liberty will have 35 games on national TV, with 33 games for the Aces. </p>
<p>"The WNBA is coming off a landmark season in 2025, one that was defined by incredible basketball and countless memorable performances," WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert said in a statement. "With an incredible 2026 draft and so many notable free agency signings and trades now giving way to the start of the season, the WNBA's unprecedented lineup of linear and streaming partners will shine a light on must-see matchups, and the remarkable skills the WNBA's stars provide each time they step on the court."</p>
<p>This upcoming season will be the first under the new media rights deal, which features new partners NBCUniversal and USA Sports.</p>
<p> </p>
<p> </p>
<p> </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 23 Apr 2026 02:02:59 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285244/WNBA-To-Broadcast-Record-216-Games-Nationally-Including-All-44-Fever-Games</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_wnba.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Adam Silver Supports Grizzlies Playing Select Games In Nashville While Backing Memphis Long-Term</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285243/Adam-Silver-Supports-Grizzlies-Playing-Select-Games-In-Nashville-While-Backing-Memphis-Long-Term</link>
      <description><p>NBA Commissioner Adam Silver expressed confidence in the Memphis Grizzlies' long-term viability Wednesday while acknowledging he would welcome a greater franchise presence in Nashville, responding directly to recent remarks by LeBron James suggesting the team relocate.</p>
<p>Speaking on Barstool's "Pardon My Take" podcast, Silver confirmed that Grizzlies governor Robert Pera has expressed no interest in leaving Memphis. Silver did, however, float the idea of the club playing occasional games in the state capital.</p>
<p>"Nashville is a city on the rise," Silver said. "If it were up to me, I'd love for them to play a few games a year in Nashville and sort of be Tennessee's team to the extent they can."</p>
<p>James sparked the discussion earlier in April during a "Bob Does Sports" appearance, stating that NBA players widely dislike visiting Memphis and that Nashville, home to the NHL's Predators and NFL's Titans, would be a more suitable destination.</p>
<p>"Go over to Nashville," James said. "They got everything."</p>
<p>Silver disputed James' characterization of player sentiment toward Memphis.</p>
<p>"Players I talk to all the time like playing in Memphis," Silver said. "Memphis has been a great market, historically for the NBA, amazing history in that city, amazing culture."</p>
<p>There is regional precedent for split home schedules. The San Antonio Spurs have played two regular-season games at Austin's Moody Center in each of the past four seasons as part of their I-35 Series. However, Memphis and Nashville are separated by 213 miles, compared to just 77 miles between San Antonio and Austin.</p>
<p>The Grizzlies' FedExForum lease expires after the 2028-29 season. The facility opened in 2004, three years after the franchise relocated from Vancouver.</p>
<p>"From my standpoint, there's no reason why the Memphis Grizzlies can't be successful," Silver said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 21:45:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285243/Adam-Silver-Supports-Grizzlies-Playing-Select-Games-In-Nashville-While-Backing-Memphis-Long-Term</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_memphisgrizzlies.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Raptors Parent Company Plans $18.25 Billion Sports Entity After MLSE Buyout</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285242/Raptors-Parent-Company-Plans-$1825-Billion-Sports-Entity-After-MLSE-Buyout</link>
      <description><p>Rogers Communications, majority owner of the Toronto Raptors through its 75% stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, announced plans Wednesday to complete its full acquisition of MLSE and consolidate its sports holdings into a single entity valued at more than $18.25 billion.</p>
<p>CEO Tony Staffieri confirmed on the company's first-quarter earnings call that Rogers expects to purchase the remaining 25% MLSE minority interest, held by Larry Tanenbaum's Kilmer Sports Ventures, in the second half of 2025.</p>
<p>"Following the close, we plan to combine our assets into one of the most significant sports ownership, media and entertainment entities globally," Staffieri said.</p>
<p>Rogers acquired BCE's 37.5% MLSE stake in 2025, bringing its ownership to 75%. MLSE controls the NBA's Raptors, NHL's Maple Leafs, and MLS's Toronto FC. Rogers separately owns MLB's Blue Jays.</p>
<p>The company intends to bring in minority investors for the consolidated sports group, using proceeds to reduce debt. CFO Glenn Brandt said the recapitalization process is expected to conclude in late 2026 or early 2027.</p>
<p>Current Sportico valuations place the Raptors at $4.66 billion, the Maple Leafs at $4.25 billion, the Blue Jays at $2.9 billion, and Toronto FC at $730 million, a combined $12.5 billion. The broader entity would also incorporate Rogers' Sportsnet media assets.</p>
<p>Rogers stock surged nearly 14% Wednesday, closing at $37.48, following guidance of reduced capital spending and stronger free cash flow. First-quarter revenue and earnings largely met analyst expectations.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 21:38:36 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285242/Raptors-Parent-Company-Plans-$1825-Billion-Sports-Entity-After-MLSE-Buyout</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_torontoraptors.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Aspiration Founder Cooperated With NBA's Kawhi Leonard Salary Cap Investigation</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285241/Aspiration-Founder-Cooperated-With-NBAs-Kawhi-Leonard-Salary-Cap-Investigation</link>
      <description><p>Joseph Sanberg, the Aspiration co-founder facing federal sentencing April 27, provided substantial cooperation to the NBA's ongoing salary cap circumvention investigation involving Kawhi Leonard and the Los Angeles Clippers, the league's lead investigator confirmed in a court letter.</p>
<p>David Anders of Wachtell Lipton stated in an April 17 filing that Sanberg sat for two in-person interviews, supplied relevant documents, and delivered information consistent with the league's broader review of evidence.</p>
<p>"Mr. Sanberg's cooperation substantially assisted our investigation, including our ability to develop a more complete understanding of key events," Anders wrote.</p>
<p>The NBA launched its probe in September 2025 following reports that Leonard's $28 million Aspiration sponsorship was structured to circumvent the salary cap. Clippers owner Steve Ballmer had invested $50 million in the company in 2021, with the club separately announcing a $300 million, 23-year sponsorship agreement. Ballmer made an additional $10 million investment in March 2023.</p>
<p>Sanberg previously pleaded guilty to two counts of wire fraud, with prosecutors alleging he defrauded investors of $248 million. Each count carries a maximum 20-year sentence. His attorneys are seeking leniency, describing him as motivated by purpose rather than personal gain.</p>
<p>In a separate court letter, Ballmer's attorney stated the Clippers owner lost his entire $60 million Aspiration investment after the company declared bankruptcy in 2025, and has suffered extensive reputational damage. The Clippers lost nearly all $300 million in sponsorship payments and more than $20 million held in escrow.</p>
<p>Ballmer has additionally been named in a civil lawsuit by 11 former Aspiration investors. He has denied all allegations and sought dismissal of the case.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 21:14:52 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285241/Aspiration-Founder-Cooperated-With-NBAs-Kawhi-Leonard-Salary-Cap-Investigation</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Leonard_Kawhi_lac_250423.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Keldon Johnson Wins 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man Of The Year Award</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285240/Keldon-Johnson-Wins-2025-26-NBA-Sixth-Man-Of-The-Year-Award</link>
      <description><p>San Antonio Spurs guard-forward Keldon Johnson has won the 2025-26 <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Sixth-Man-of-the-Year/3">NBA Sixth Man of the Year Award</a>, earning the John Havlicek Trophy after a standout season off the bench.</p>
<p>Johnson averaged 13.2 points and 5.4 rebounds in 23.3 minutes per game, becoming the only player in the league to appear in all 82 games as a reserve. He shot 51.9% from the field, his most efficient mark since his rookie campaign in 2019-20.</p>
<p>His 1,081 bench points set a San Antonio franchise record and ranked second league-wide. The Spurs finished with the NBA's second-best record at 62-20.</p>
<p>A panel of 100 global media voters determined the award winner. Miami Heat forward Jamie Jaquez Jr. finished second with 34 first-place votes and 51 second-place votes. Tim Hardaway Jr. placed third, followed by Naz Reid and Ajay Mitchell.</p>
<p>Johnson becomes the second Spur to claim the honor, joining Manu Ginobili, who won the award in 2007-08.</p>
<p>To qualify for consideration, a player must have come off the bench in more games than he started during the regular season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 21:08:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285240/Keldon-Johnson-Wins-2025-26-NBA-Sixth-Man-Of-The-Year-Award</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Johnson_Keldon_sas_211018.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Steve Kerr Meeting With Media Agents Ahead Of Warriors' Decision</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285239/Steve-Kerr-Meeting-With-Media-Agents-Ahead-Of-Warriors-Decision</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr has been meeting with media agents as his Golden State Warriors contract approaches expiration, with broadcasting, front office work, and a return to coaching all remaining possibilities, sources told Front Office Sports.</p>
<p>Kerr has not yet settled on a next step. A spokesperson for his coaching agency, Priority Sports, declined to comment. Sources indicated the media meetings have taken place over several years and centered on broader long-term projects.</p>
<p>All three current NBA broadcast partners, ESPN, NBC, and Amazon, would pursue conversations with Kerr should he choose to re-enter the media space, per the report.</p>
<p>Kerr brings rare credentials to any potential broadcasting role. He won nine NBA championships total, five as a player, including three with Michael Jordan's Chicago Bulls and two with the San Antonio Spurs, and four more as Golden State's head coach.</p>
<p>Kerr has also already demonstrated elite ability behind the microphone. Kerr served as TNT's lead color analyst in two separate stints alongside play-by-play voice Marv Albert, from 2003-07 and again from 2010-14, with a term as Phoenix Suns general manager between the two runs.</p>
<p>Those broadcasts showcased sharp tactical analysis, polished timing, and a sense of humor, qualities that would make him a sought-after addition to any NBA rights holder's talent roster.</p>
<p>Kerr would reenter broadcasting with an even higher profile and direct familiarity with the modern game.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 20:51:39 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285239/Steve-Kerr-Meeting-With-Media-Agents-Ahead-Of-Warriors-Decision</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_240113.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Darius Acuff Jr. Declares For 2026 NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285238/Darius-Acuff-Jr-Declares-For-2026-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>Arkansas guard Darius Acuff Jr. announced Wednesday on ESPN's "NBA Today" that he is entering the 2026 NBA Draft. The 6-foot-3 point guard is currently ranked the sixth-best prospect available, per ESPN's Jeremy Woo.</p>
<p>"With that being said, I'm going to be declaring for the 2026 NBA draft," Acuff said.</p>
<p>Acuff had a record-setting freshman campaign with the Razorbacks, earning SEC Player of the Year honors and a spot on the Associated Press All-America first team. He averaged 23.5 points, 6.4 rebounds, and 3.1 assists while shooting 44% from three-point range, leading Arkansas to the Sweet 16.</p>
<p>The announcement continues a notable pattern under coach John Calipari. Derrick Rose, John Wall, De'Aaron Fox, and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander are among the lottery picks who developed under Calipari at Memphis and Kentucky. Acuff cited that track record as a factor in his decision to sign with Arkansas.</p>
<p>NBA Hall of Famer Allen Iverson also played a formative role in Acuff's development and style of play. The two have built a relationship in recent months.</p>
<p>"We've got a lot of similarities. He's one of the reasons I got cornrows, so he inspired me a lot," Acuff said. "He always told me to just play every game like it's your last."</p>
<p>Acuff said watching Tuesday's Los Angeles Lakers-Houston Rockets playoff game reinforced his ambitions at the next level.</p>
<p>"I see myself there, for sure. That's one of my goals," he said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 17:08:25 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285238/Darius-Acuff-Jr-Declares-For-2026-NBA-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Acuff_Darius_ark_260316.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Austin Reaves Closer To Returning Than Luka Doncic</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285236/Austin-Reaves-Closer-To-Returning-Than-Luka-Doncic</link>
      <description><p>The Los Angeles Lakers have taken a 2-0 series lead over the Houston Rockets, despite being shorthanded. Now, Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves are starting the process of ramping up for a return. Reaves is closer to being able to play than Doncic.</p>
<p>"The sense around the Lakers is that Austin Reaves is actually the one that's further along than Luka Doncic in their respective rehab processes," ESPN NBA insider Shams Charania said. "I am told Austin Reaves has started one-on-one on-court work. The next step for him is to continue to go through the progressions of three-on-three, five-on-five.</p>
<p>"Remember, early April, he had a four-to-six-week timetable. So theoretically that puts him on track (to return) late in this series (or) potentially early in the next series."</p>
<p>Doncic has started some light and limited on-court work. There's been no update for Doncic's timeframe to return, but the Lakers do not expect the superstar to return until the second round, should they advance that far.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:09:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285236/Austin-Reaves-Closer-To-Returning-Than-Luka-Doncic</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Reaves_Austin_lal_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Marcus Smart Dominates Kevin Durant, Lakers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Rockets</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285235/Marcus-Smart-Dominates-Kevin-Durant-Lakers-Take-2-0-Series-Lead-Over-Rockets</link>
      <description><p>Marcus Smart delivered a standout two-way performance Tuesday, holding Kevin Durant in check while scoring 25 points to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 101-94 Game 2 victory over the Houston Rockets.</p>
<p>Durant, returning from a right knee injury that sidelined him for Game 1, finished with 23 points. However, he shot just 1-for-3 and committed three of his playoff career-worst nine turnovers across 18 possessions guarded by Smart, per ESPN Research.</p>
<p>Smart finished with 8-for-13 shooting, five 3-pointers, seven assists, and five steals. His fifth three-pointer, with 2:23 remaining, extended a five-point advantage to eight and effectively sealed the outcome.</p>
<p>"Smart, he just had a killer game today," said Lakers coach JJ Redick.</p>
<p>LeBron James paced Los Angeles with 28 points, eight rebounds, and seven assists. James praised Smart's composure, noting his value with Austin Reaves and Luka Doncic both sidelined.</p>
<p>"He's not afraid of the moment. He's always been assigned some of the best players that [have] ever played this game in his career. So, to have someone like that it just brings a lot of composure to our team," James said.</p>
<p>Redick confirmed Reaves has begun on-court workouts while recovering from a Grade 2 oblique strain. Doncic, sidelined with a Grade 2 left hamstring strain, has not yet started his return-to-play progression. Neither player has a set return date.</p>
<p>Houston shot 40.4% and has scored below 100 points in consecutive games. The series shifts to Houston for Game 3 on Friday <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">with the Rockets 9.5 point favorites</a>. Te Rockets haven't won <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Houston-Rockets/10/Playoff-History">a playoff series</a> since 2020 when they defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder in seven games in the first round.</p>
<p>Smart, a 2021-22 <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Defensive-Player-of-the-Year/2">Defensive Player of the Year</a> who reached the 2022 NBA Finals with Boston, reflected on his resurgence following injury-hampered stints in Memphis and Washington.</p>
<p>"I'm very grateful to be doing this," Smart said. "I thank God every day, because I could have been out the league, right?"</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 07:33:28 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285235/Marcus-Smart-Dominates-Kevin-Durant-Lakers-Take-2-0-Series-Lead-Over-Rockets</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Smart_Marcus_lal_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Scoot Henderson On Scoring 31 Points In Blazers' Game 2 Win: 'Attack, Get Downhill'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285234/Scoot-Henderson-On-Scoring-31-Points-In-Blazers-Game-2-Win-Attack-Get-Downhill</link>
      <description><p>Scoot Henderson scored 31 points as the Portland Trail Blazers defeated the San Antonio Spurs 106-103 to win Game 2 and even the series.</p>
<p>Henderson, who did not score 30 points in the regular season, finished 11-of-17 from the field.</p>
<p>"I'm so blessed to even play in a situation like this," Henderson said. "Playoffs, third year, younger team, with great vets. The picture couldn't have painted itself better."</p>
<p>Henderson added that he was focused on being aggressive and getting downhill.</p>
<p>"It's just a feeling," he said. "I was just telling myself the night before, 'Attack.' They can't mess with you. That was my goal to come in here and attack, get downhill, play hard, and whatever happens, happens."</p>
<p>Jrue Holiday added 16 points, with Deni Avdija finishing with 14. </p>
<p>Stephon Castle lead the Spurs with 18 points.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285229/Victor-Wembanyama-Suffers-Concussion-After-Hitting-Floor-In-Game-2-Loss">Victor Wembanyama left the game</a> in the second quarter with a concussion.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 02:02:51 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285234/Scoot-Henderson-On-Scoring-31-Points-In-Blazers-Game-2-Win-Attack-Get-Downhill</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Henderson_Scoot_por_231028.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Napheesa Collier Out Until Early June</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285233/Napheesa-Collier-Out-Until-Early-June</link>
      <description><p>Minnesota Lynx star Napheesa Collier will be out until early June after undergoing ankle surgery on March 24, the team announced on Tuesday.</p>
<p>Collier also had surgery on her right ankle in January.</p>
<p>Collier, who signed a one-year supermax contract to return to the Lynx, will be an unrestricted free agent next season.</p>
<p>"I'm so focused on, right now, with my injury and this year and winning a championship," Collier said. "That is what I'm doing, so getting back with our amazing staff, playing with our amazing players and just focused on this year and winning."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 01:53:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285233/Napheesa-Collier-Out-Until-Early-June</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_wnba.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Tiago Splitter On Blazers' Coaching Rumors: 'Just Trying To Be A Pro'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285232/Tiago-Splitter-On-Blazers-Coaching-Rumors-Just-Trying-To-Be-A-Pro</link>
      <description><p>Portland Trail Blazers head coach Tiago Splitter said he was trying to remain professional with rumors of new owner Tom Dundon <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285216/Tiago-Splitter-Remains-Leading-Candidate-For-Blazers-Head-Coaching-Job">conducting an extensive coaching search</a> with the team in the playoffs. </p>
<p>"Just trying to be a pro," Splitter said before the Blazers' 106-103 win over the Spurs in Game 2. "[I] try to focus on my locker room and my staff to stay and think about basketball. Same way when I got the job and all the stuff that was going on."</p>
<p>Splitter was reportedly given a raise from his assistant coach salary after taking over from Chauncey Billups and discussed a long-term deal during the season, sources told ESPN.</p>
<p>The offer was far below the standard NBA coaching salary, and talks were placed on hold until after the season.</p>
<p>The Oregonian reported last week that Dundon wants to hire a coach in the range of $1 million per year.</p>
<p>Splitter added that he advised his players to stay off social media ahead of the playoffs.</p>
<p>"A couple coaches that I had gave me that advice. I know it's hard. Social media is part of our lives, but trying to just focus on basketball."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 22 Apr 2026 01:48:23 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285232/Tiago-Splitter-On-Blazers-Coaching-Rumors-Just-Trying-To-Be-A-Pro</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Splitter_Tiago_por_251025.jpg" />
   </item>
